summary: Iteration 4 (v2.2) testing for Alyssa Driver Checkpoint: Album foto 4-stage (asal/kapal/tujuan/dokumen) + Public tracking page. Backend: 37/37 pytest passed (25 regression + 12 new iter-4). Frontend: All 3 scenarios pass (driver album tabs, tracking page, routing).

issues: Value 'Belum' visually appears oversized/overflowing on the 3rd tile at 414w viewport — likely due to trk-tile-val font-size being too large for word 'Belum'. Consider truncating or scaling text. Not blocking; content still legible.
Footer still labelled 'v2.0 Driver Checkpoint' but backend version is now v2.2. Minor cosmetic inconsistency.

action_items: (Optional cosmetic) Bump driver footer label from 'v2.0' to 'v2.2' in DriverCheckpoint.jsx line ~725.
(Optional cosmetic) Reduce font-size or use ellipsis for trk-tile-val when value is text (e.g., 'Belum' / 'Lengkap') so it doesn't visually overflow on 414w viewport.
critical_code_review_comments: DriverCheckpoint.jsx is now ~785 lines — getting large; split Album section, Handover section, and TahapCard into separate files for maintainability.
Backend public_trip() correctly whitelists fields and explicitly excludes xendit/odoo_synced/cair/sop_read/t1-t3/bonus/driver_id (verified test_34).
Album upload uses extension whitelist correctly; PDF rejected for non-dokumen stages with 400 (verified parametrized test_30 across asal/kapal/tujuan).
init_trip backfills album field for legacy docs on re-init (verified test_27) — good non-breaking migration approach.
Public tracking page auto-refreshes via setInterval(30s) with proper cleanup on unmount (cancelled flag + clearInterval) — clean React effect.
CustomerTracking.jsx and DriverCheckpoint.jsx share ALBUM_STAGES/stageLabel/stageIcon — could be extracted to a shared util module to avoid duplication.
No max upload size enforced at backend (still carryover from earlier iterations); frontend limits 15MB but server doesn't. Low priority since static.

delete_album_photo uses $pull which is idempotent on missing IDs and returns 200 — be aware: deleting a non-existent photo_id on an existing trip silently succeeds (no 404). Acceptable for UX but worth noting.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_driver_checkpoint.py (fixed test_01 to expect v=2.2; added test_26..test_35 for album + public tracking — 12 new tests via parametrize)
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: MongoDB trips: TRIP-I4-*, TRIP-LEG-*, TRIP-FE-ALBUM (driver named 'Tester', nopol 'B 1234 XX', route Jakarta-Surabaya). All accessible via GET /api/trips/{id} and GET /api/public/trips/{id}.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_driver_checkpoint.py now covers 37 tests including iter-4 album + public tracking. Key iter-4 endpoints: POST /api/trips/{id}/album (form: stage, foto, catatan, uploaded_by) returns full trip doc; DELETE /api/trips/{id}/album/{stage}/{photo_id}; GET /api/public/trips/{id} (sanitized read-only view). Album stages = {asal, kapal, tujuan, dokumen}; only 'dokumen' accepts PDF. Frontend test trip TRIP-FE-ALBUM is seeded — reuse it for further testing. To test backfill, the test simulates by re-calling init (idempotent). Routing: '?trip=' → DriverCheckpoint, '?track=' → CustomerTracking, '/' → DriverCheckpoint (default, shows error if no trip param).
rca of the issue: No issues found. All iter-4 features (album 4-stage CRUD with PDF rule per stage, public tracking with field whitelisting, routing via query param, frontend tabs + dynamic button labels + counter pill) work as specified. Backend v2.2 confirmed.
